_bundled_note: Local copy for offline validation. Canonical source: governance-evidence/decision-event-schema
title: Decision Event
description: The minimal unit of governance evidence that enables post-incident reconstruction of automated decisions. Each optional property resists a specific governance artifact degradation type.

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the Decision Event Schema this event conforms to. Enables forward compatibility as the schema evolves.

decision_id.description: Unique identifier for this decision event. May be a UUID or a human-readable slug.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the decision was executed.

decision_type.description: Whether the decision was made by a human, an automated system, or a combination.

decision_context.description: Available inputs, alternatives considered, and constraints operative at the time of decision. Resists Content Staleness degradation.

available_inputs.description: Data sources and signals available to the decision-maker at the time of decision.

alternatives_considered.description: Decision alternatives that were evaluated before the final choice.

constraints_operative.description: Regulatory, technical, or organizational constraints that bounded the decision space.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).

decision_logic.description: Rule or model version, parameters, and thresholds at the time of decision. Resists Schema Drift degradation.

rule_version.description: Version identifier of the rule set or model used.

parameters.description: Key parameters that governed the decision.

thresholds.description: Threshold values that triggered or influenced the decision.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).

decision_boundary.description: Contributing subsystems and external dependencies involved in the decision. Resists Coverage Erosion degradation.

contributing_subsystems.description: Internal subsystems that contributed to or were affected by the decision.

external_dependencies.description: External systems, data feeds, or services the decision depended on.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).

decision_quality_indicators.description: Confidence scores, uncertainty flags, and ground truth availability. Resists Metric Erosion degradation.

confidence_score.description: System-reported confidence in the decision (0.0 to 1.0). Null when no score was computed.

uncertainty_flags.description: Conditions flagged as uncertain at the time of decision.

ground_truth_available.description: Whether ground truth data was available at the time of decision.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).

human_override_record.description: Override decision, independence assessment, and basis for deviation. Resists Override Accumulation degradation.

override_decision.description: What the human override changed relative to the automated recommendation. Null when override was structurally impossible or absent by design.

independence_assessment.description: Whether the override was made independently or under organizational pressure. Null when no override occurred.

basis_for_deviation.description: The stated rationale for deviating from the automated recommendation. Null when no override occurred.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).

temporal_metadata.description: Timestamps tracking the gap between decision, evidence availability, and ground truth arrival. Resists Content Staleness degradation (via temporal evidence decay).

evidence_availability_timestamp.description: When evidence about this decision became available for review. Null when evidence availability cannot be established.

ground_truth_arrival_timestamp.description: When ground truth about the decision outcome became known.

_recoverability_note.description: Post-hoc annotation classifying the recoverability status of this property (e.g., recoverable, partially recoverable, structurally unfillable, opaque).
